problem.title: Find Median from Data Stream
problem.description: The median is the middle value in an ordered integer list.
assumptions: findMedian returns exact median (average of two middle values if even count).

example.explanation: Use two heaps: max-heap for left half, min-heap for right half. Median is the top(s).

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Two Heaps [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Maintain two heaps: a max-heap for the lower half and a min-heap for the upper half of the data stream. Every new number is first pushed to the max-heap, then the max-heap's top is moved to the min-heap to enforce the partition. If the heaps become unbalanced in size, an element is moved back so the max-heap has at most one more element, giving O(1) median retrieval.

complexity.time: O(log n) for add, O(1) for find
